summary:
The United States and Iran exchanged new rounds of attacks overnight, escalating the conflict over the Strait of Hormuz. The U.S. launched multiple strikes on Iranian targets, while Iran fired at commercial vessels in the key waterway. President Trump denied Iran's claim to have closed the strait. The fighting threatens to disrupt global oil shipments through a passage that carries about 20% of the world's crude.

Senator Lindsey Graham of South Carolina died at 71 after what his spokesman called a brief and sudden heart-related illness. Graham was a close ally of President Trump and a senior Republican voice on foreign policy. In Bangkok, a fire tore through a bar in the Chatuchak district, killing at least 27 people and injuring over 70. Authorities report 45 still missing as rescue efforts continue.

China became the second country after the United States to recover a reusable orbital rocket booster at sea. The Long March-10B's first stage landed on a floating platform using a novel technique. The achievement signals China's growing ambitions in space technology. Meanwhile, in Ukraine, ground robots are reshaping the war with Russia. Originally used as supply mules, they now evacuate wounded soldiers, hold trenches, and even engage in combat, according to Ukrainian forces.

The UK recorded more than 2,700 excess deaths during record heatwaves in May and June, when temperatures hit 37.7°C. Officials warn that extreme heat poses a growing public health risk. In Nigeria, suspected armed groups killed at least 27 people in separate attacks on communities in Benue and Plateau states. Residents are calling for urgent government intervention to stop the violence.

headlines.title: U.S. and Iran Trade New Rounds of Attacks in Fight Over Strait of Hormuz
headlines.summary: The United States launched multiple strikes on Iranian targets while Iran fired at commercial vessels. President Trump denied Iran's claim to have closed the Strait of Hormuz, escalating the conflict.

headlines.title: Fire Tears Through Bangkok Bar, Kills at Least 27
headlines.summary: A fire engulfed a bar in Bangkok's Chatuchak district, killing at least 27 people and injuring over 70. Authorities report 45 still missing as rescue efforts continue.

headlines.title: US senator and close Trump ally Lindsey Graham dies after 'brief and sudden illness'
headlines.summary: Senator Lindsey Graham of South Carolina died at 71 due to a heart-related illness, his spokesman said. Graham was a key Trump ally and senior Republican figure.

headlines.title: China joins US in reusable orbital rocket capability
headlines.summary: China successfully recovered its Long March-10B rocket booster at sea, becoming the second nation after the US to achieve reusable orbital rocket technology.

headlines.title: More than 2,700 deaths in UK linked to May, June heatwaves
headlines.summary: The UK recorded over 2,700 excess deaths during record heatwaves in May and June, with temperatures reaching 37.7°C, highlighting the health impact of extreme heat.

headlines.title: Nigeria: Benue, Plateau Communities Hit By Fresh Attacks, 27 Killed
headlines.summary: Suspected armed groups killed at least 27 people in separate attacks on communities in Benue and Plateau states, prompting calls for government intervention.

headlines.title: A Robot Army Remakes Ground Warfare in Ukraine
headlines.summary: Ukraine increasingly uses ground robots for supply, evacuation, and combat. The robot army remakes battlefield tactics in the war with Russia.
